page.title: Workforce Studio
page.intro.lead: Design and inspect the agent system. The
page.intro.mid: view is your declarative design; the
page.intro.tail: view is what's running right now.
origin.core.label: core
origin.builtin.label: built-in
origin.tenant.label: tenant
origin.core.title: Provided by the host (no plugin involved).
origin.builtin.title: From a plugin shipped with this Tianshu install.
origin.tenant.title: From a plugin installed per-tenant.
plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.activeHint: (active = contributing to the agent right now)
plugins.activeHintTitle: Every plugin currently activated for this tenant. Tools, skills and prompt fragments come from these.
plugins.empty: No plugins discovered for this tenant.
mainAgent.title: Main agent
mainAgent.rendered.hint: Rendered prompt the model would receive on its next turn ({size}).
block.editable.title: You can edit the underlying source.
block.readOnly.title: Managed by the host or a plugin — not editable.
workers.title: Workers
tools.title: Tools ({n})
skills.title: Skills ({n})
solution.confirm.delete: Delete solution "{slug}"? This cannot be undone.
ide.refresh.title: Refresh solutions
ide.extract.title: Extract current reality into a new named solution
ide.import.title: Import a solution from a .solution.json file (creates a new solution)
ide.export.title: Export this solution to a .solution.json file
ide.activate.title: Make this the live solution: write its config into the running system + mark it active. Takes effect on the next agent turn.
activate.modal.title: Activate solution
activate.modal.body.tail: the live solution? Its main-agent config, workers and plugin enable-set are written into the running system; the agent picks them up on its next turn. Any previously-active solution is superseded.
inspector.title: Inspector
inspector.diff.title: Diff vs reality
inspector.rendered.title: Rendered preview
inspector.blurb.plugins: Plugin set drives the available tools ({tools}), skills ({skills}) and prompt fragments below. {enabled} of {total} enabled.
inspector.blurb.main: Main agent: {blocks} prompt blocks, {tools} tools, {skills} skills, {workers} workers.
inspector.blurb.tenantPrompt: The tenant prompt is the editable body of the main-agent system prompt for this solution.
inspector.blurb.override.on: Overridden for this solution. The host default is replaced by your text below.
inspector.blurb.override.off: Using the host default. Override it to customise this block for the solution.
inspector.blurb.fragment: A custom prompt fragment appended to the main-agent prompt.
editor.mainTools.title: 🔧 Tools
editor.mainTools.sub: Tools available to the main agent. Plugin / host tools are locked in; exclude tenant-owned tools you don't want.
editor.mainSkills.title: 📚 Skills
editor.mainSkills.sub: Skills available to the main agent. Plugin / host skills are locked in; exclude tenant-owned skills you don't want.
editor.none.sub: Select a node from the Explorer.
editor.field.name: Name
editor.metadata.saveApplyHint: writes this solution to disk (no runtime effect).
editor.metadata.applyHint: writes it into the running system — main-agent prompt / skills / tools + worker files take effect on the next agent turn. Plugin enable/disable is not applied in this phase.
editor.metadata.currentMirror.tail: in the Solutions list to create an editable named solution.
editor.plugins.title: 🧩 Plugins
editor.plugins.sub: Which plugins this solution activates. Plugins decide what tools, skills and prompt fragments the rest of the solution can use.
editor.plugins.included.title: Included — click to exclude from this solution.
editor.plugins.excluded.title: Excluded — click to include.
editor.tenantPrompt.title: 📝 Tenant prompt
editor.tenantPrompt.sub: The main-agent prompt text for this solution.
editor.tenantPrompt.placeholder: Main-agent prompt text for this solution. Leave empty for none.
editor.override.sub: Host-provided block. Override it for this solution, or keep the host default.
editor.fragment.title: ➕ Custom fragment
editor.fragment.sub: Extra text injected into the main-agent prompt.
editor.fragment.field.title: Title
editor.fragment.bodyPlaceholder: Fragment text injected into the main agent prompt…
editor.workers.title: 👥 Workers
editor.workers.sub: Include or exclude a worker here; expand it in the Explorer to edit its prompt / tools / skills. Excluded workers are dropped from the applied solution.
editor.workers.excluded.title: Excluded from this solution — click to include.
editor.workers.included.title: Included — click to exclude this worker from the solution.
editor.worker.notFound.title: Worker
editor.worker.soul.title: 📝 {name} — SOUL.md
editor.worker.soul.placeholder: Worker SOUL.md — the worker's persona + rules.
editor.worker.eb.title: ⚙ {name} — Execution bias
editor.worker.eb.sub: Host behaviour rules for this worker. Override to replace them just for this worker; reset to fall back to the host default.
editor.worker.tools.title: 🔧 {name} — Tools
editor.worker.skills.title: 📚 {name} — Skills
editor.worker.root.sub: Worker fields. Expand sub-nodes in the Explorer for SOUL, tools, and skills.
editor.worker.excluded.title: Excluded from this solution — click to include.
editor.worker.included.title: Included — click to exclude this worker from the solution.
editor.worker.field.name: Name
resource.locked.title: Contributed by a plugin or the host — always included.
resource.locked.label: locked
resource.row.included.title: Currently included — click to exclude from this solution.
resource.row.excluded.title: Currently excluded — click to include again.
manifest.description: Inspect + export the tenant's main agent and every worker agent — system prompts, available tools, skills — into a single downloadable bundle. Phase 1 is read-only: editing + import land in later phases.
